description: In `scale_predictors()`, `lapply(df[num_cols], scale)` assigns the result of `base::scale()` directly back into the data frame. `scale()` returns a matrix with `dimnames` and `'scaled:center'`/`'scaled:scale'` attributes. When stored in a data frame column, this creates a one-column matrix rather than a plain numeric vector, breaking downstream functions that expect atomic columns (e.g. `stats::lm()` raises 'variable lengths differ'). Fix `scale_predictors()` by wrapping each `scale()` result in `as.numeric()` so the attributes are dropped.
